From Solo AI to Social AI 
Designing Copilot For Social Spread & Engagement
MAI Discovery Research 
Ming-Li Chai & Rachele Bejamin 
5-8-2026

AI

Why should MAI care?
[Growth] 
Sharing is a growth and virality engine
[Engagement] 
Social use deepens solo engagement
[Experience Innovation] 
New use cases and high value vertical scenarios
[Brand] 
Opportunity to pre-empt “social AI” in life and work

Discovery questions:
1 → Many 
How does AI use spread form one to many?  
Many → Better 
How might AI help groups become better together?

HEAT SENSING: Quant
DEPTH & DISOVERY : Qual

fluency (M = 3.43)

and “joint-use” opportunities

partners, special interest groups, classroom, 
project teams, service providers)
Yielding in-depth insights about 
social AI momentum, hesitations 
and high-value scenarios

AI Sharing : User stories 
Megan gets lab results back
Social AI behavior: Solo use → shares 
findings across different audiences
Momentum: AI reduces uncertainty
Hesitation: resistance from healthcare team, 
family stress
Cody creates a liquidity report
Social AI behavior: Solo research and PPT 
creation → seeks targeted feedback
Momentum: AI helps build the story arc
Hesitation: Loss of his own voice
Chris plans a road trip to Montana
Social AI behavior: Solo search → shares 
options
Momentum: High excitement + validation
Hesitation: Limited group input

Solo → Social AI Diffusion Loop

Social Discomfort
Fear of judgment, stigma of using AI, not wanting to seem lazy or incompetent
 Tool Friction
No easy way to share — copy-pasting, screenshotting, switching between apps
 Inauthenticity
AI output doesn't sound like "me" — sharing it feels dishonest or impersonal
 Lack of Response
Sharing into a void — no reaction, no engagement, effort feels wasted
Difficult Social Discourse
Debates devolve, opinions clash, AI adds complexity instead of clarity
Lack of Commitment
Others won't follow through — plans die, no accountability, coordination tax
Excitement of Discovery
Finding something genuinely useful or surprising — the urge to say "look at this!"
Benefit to Self & Others
Sharing creates value for both sides — saves time, solves problems, builds connection
Ease of Curation
When it's easy to reshape AI output into something personal and shareable
Positive Reaction
Getting engagement, appreciation, or follow-up — social validation of sharing
Mutual Interest
Shared context makes sharing natural — couples, hobby groups, friend squads
Commitment to Cooperation
When both parties invest — joint projects, shared goals, persistent accountability

Trust in solo is a pre-condition to trust in social
[circles of sharing & permissions]
“Will AI tell everyone my secrets?"
[accurate, tone]
“What will others think of my AI use?"
[authentic voice]
“Will AI represent me poorly?"
[neutral third party]
“Will the group accept the AI?"
[group-level controls]
“Will they feel comfortable with AI?"
[moderating voice]
“Will AI protect us?"
[security need]
“Is my information protected?"
[accuracy need]
"Can I trust this output?"
[sources, balanced perspectives]
“Is this information unbiased?"
Persistent hold-backs:
•
Privacy [49%], 
•
Accuracy [42%]
Emergent hold-backs: 
•
Group comfort [30%], 
•
Appearing dependent on AI [30%]

Share Better: 
Help me express and influence with confidence
Me  → You : Put it out there 
Controlled Curation
Social-able Artifact
Authentic Voice
Ubiquitous Share
Sharing today means screenshots, copy-paste, and verbal retelling, losing context, tone, or interactivity.
Select, omit, and polish before sharing, not all-
or-nothing chat dumps.
Transform AI chat into expressive, easy-
to-consume media or visual summaries.
A floating widget across every app. Tap, 
select, preview, send. No app-switching.
Output that sounds like you. Your tone, 
emphasis, and thinking made visible.
KERRI · PREGNANCY HEALTH SCARE
"Share Better → Loop In → Jump In"
Kenna's AI chat is too long and includes embarrassing questions. 
AI offers a curated summary — she selects/omits parts, exports 
to Snapchat. Kerri joins via invite link with a catch-up summary.
IRIDIAN · SKINCARE RESEARCH FOR FRIENDS
TikTok-Style Explainer Clip
Micro-needling research becomes a 20-second animated 
video in modern slang. A cartoon pen explains the procedure. 
Shareable across platforms — each recipient gets the format that 
fits.
CODY · AI-ASSISTED WORK DECKS
"80% Done" — Thought Process Visible
Cody iterates until 80% done — polished enough to react to. 
AI captures where he had reservations and where he 
committed, then routes to the right expert.
JEANNETTE · SHARING STUDY BREAKTHROUGHS
Floating Share Widget
On FaceTime, Jeannette wants to share an AI breakthrough 
instantly. A floating widget lets her select the relevant snippet, 
preview, and send — works across all AI apps.
"You just screenshotted this. Would you 
like a summary? Let me curate it — 
maybe I don't want that part included.“ 
--— Kerri
"A 20-second video — I have friends with 
a very short attention span. You curate it, 
give it a tone, and AI produces it."
— Iridian
"You can't lose the thought process 
aspect. You have to capture and 
portray it.” — Cody
"A little AI logo that follows you 
wherever you are. Not specific to Gemini 
or ChatGPT.  It works for all of them."
— Jeannette

Sense Better: 
Help me tune in to what’s important 
Many → Me :  Focus Attention
TL;DR
Theme Buckets
Timely AMA
Proactive Discovery
Groups drown in linear, unsortable, unsearchable messages. The #1 pain isn't missing AI, it's information chaos. 
Quick summaries when you return : decisions 
made, input needed. 30 seconds to same page.
Conversations auto-sorted into meaningful 
buckets, searchable by meaning, not keywords.
AI surfaces timely and serendipitous things you'd 
miss: events, opportunities, connections.
Real-time answers when the window is minutes, 
not hours. AI as live analyst cutting through noise.
LISA · PANTRY REMODEL PROJECT
Async Catch-Up
Lisa's husband logs in 3 days later. AI: "Lisa narrowed 
contractors to two. Budget is $X. She's waiting on your 
countertop input." Caught up in 30 seconds.
TONY · 35-PERSON COACHING STAFF CHAT
Smart Coaching Knowledge Hub
AI auto-sorts into buckets: run concept, pass concept — by 
play type, situation, personnel. Any coach searches by 
meaning: "What did we say about the red zone?"
PAUL · SPORTS BETTING FRIENDS · REAL-TIME ODDS
Time-Sensitive "Jump In"
Odds shift by the minute. AI surfaces real-time analysis across 
multiple sites visible to all. If no response, Paul can retract the 
share and the tip expires.
CAROL · SATURDAY CREW · GROUP OUTINGS
Proactive Event Discovery
AI notifies Carol: "Since you volunteer for Girl Scouts, did 
you know about this camping event?" She shares to the 
group. AI generates a signup sheet and nudges non-
responders.
"If  my husband takes three days to log 
in, it pulls an automatic summary of  
what happened.“ — Lisa
"Organize into buckets — run concept, 
pass concept. A library you can easily 
reference.“ — Tony
“Sports betting is very time sensitive. Down 
to the minutes. Use AI to help us w 
decision making“ - Paul
"None of  us knew about that event. It was 
written small in their booklet. AI 
announced it.“ - Carol

Interact Better: 
Help us appreciate everyone’s perspective
Me → You :  Give and take
Loop You In
Level-headed 3rd Party
Loud voices dominate, quiet ones disappear, and conflicts get swept under the rug.
Loop others into a near-final plan so they can 
react, refine, or approve.
AI stays calm when you can’t. Re-articulates 
positions, provides private + joint solutions.
“Hey I think it got it down to a final 
plan. Look these over. Anything you 
want to change?” - Cassandra
”Sometimes an outside voice, like a 
therapist.  Using this as a moderator tool 
is going to give you a level-headed 
perspective." — Cassandra
CASSANDRA · MARRIED COUPLE TRIP PLANNING
Loop You In
Cassandra whittles down the trip plan solo, then alerts her 
husband: "Look these over." He can add prompts for what he 
wants to change — in his own words, so she doesn't misinterpret.
CASSANDRA · MARRIED COUPLE - SENSITIVE 
DISAGREEMENTS
AI as a Relationship Mediator
Both enter perspectives. AI confirms: "Is this what you're trying to 
say?" Then re-articulates in de-escalated language. Private 
solutions AND joint summary. Adjustable filter levels.
Async Brainstorm
“Two people bouncing ideas off each 
other, multiple perspectives, instantly 
we hey a compare-contrast.“ — Terry
Bounce ideas off each other and the bot with 
compare-contrast of perspectives.
CRAFTYBEAR · GARDEN PLANNING WITH BOYFRIEND
Async Idea Drop + Compare-Contrast
Either person drops an idea anytime. AI analyzes it, compares it 
to the other's input, and has it ready whenever the other 
person's ready to have their conversation. No 37-message 
scroll required.
Tiered Participation
Progressive tiers : lurk, vote, suggest, assert. Quiet 
voices counted, loud ones balanced.
“Tune-in is like lurking. Chime-in is an 
upvote or downvote. Jump in is invested. 
Opt-in is to confirm.” - Johnathan
JOHNATHAN · 30-PERSON EXTENDED FAMILY
Progressive Engagement Tiers
AI generates 3 options. Five tiers: Tune in → Chime in → Weigh 
in → Jump in → Opt-in . Time-boxed phases. Diverse budgetss 
respected.

Collaborate Better: 
Help us accomplish a shared outcome
We → Outcome : Better together
Group Coordination
Family PM
Joint POV
Shared Idea Space
One person bears the coordination burden while others disengage. Group projects scatter across apps and AI chats. There's no 
persistent "brain."
AI weaves multiple perspectives into one cohesive 
output.  Each role contributes expertise.
IRIDIAN · COMMUNITY HEALTH TEAM
Cohesive Multi-Perspective Presentation
Nurses, community health workers, insurance staff — each 
sees the issue differently. AI synthesizes all perspectives into 
one unified deck without losing nuance.
“If  there’s anything I hate doing it’s 
coordinating.  Map out decision matrix 
and guide through the process with a 
lot of  people’s inputs“ — Chris
“Any family project. Everyone can see it.  
How-to steps, with memory and task 
automation.” — Paul
"It gets everybody's perspective and 
generate one cohesive presentation."
— Iridian
“We both can be very forgetful, so having 
the outlet , a collaborative thing to brain-
dump and the bot respond to us."
— Terri
AI as event coordinator — maps out decision trees, 
collects everyone's inputs, builds itineraries with 
links and costs.
Shared AI on the fridge — voice-activated, step-
by-step guidance, with memory and task 
automation for the whole household.
A collaborative brain-dump space where both 
people's ideas are captured, compared, and 
remembered across sessions.
CHRIS · 5-FRIEND FLORIDA WEDDING TRIP
AI Travel Agent for the Group
Chris sends a link: "Get in there, put in whatever food 
you're interested in, whatever activities." AI considers 
weather, budget, constraints — builds an itinerary with 
estimated costs and individual booking links.
PAUL · MULTI-GENERATIONAL HOUSEHOLD
Household Manager on the Smart Fridge
Voice-activated — anyone walks up and talks. No app 
barrier for older family members. AI tracks decisions (shingle 
color vote), notifies everyone, maps out steps: mortgage 
first, then realtor, then inspector.
CRAFTYBEAR · GARDEN PLANNING WITH BOYFRIEND
Persistent Memory Across Seasons
Either person drops an idea anytime — AI captures it, 
responds, compares to the other's input. "Two seasons ago 
you tried X, don't suggest it again." The project has a brain 
that never forgets.

3 Future scenario demos

The present, and the future, of AI is Social
[Growth] 
Design sharing as a first-class experience, not a workaround
[Engagement] 
Build for the full loop of solo → social engagement journey
[Experience] 
Focus on high-value scenarios where people naturally involve others
[Brand]
Lead in defining what ‘Social AI’ means in both life and work
